title: Change the engine in your vehicle
description: Tell the Barbados Licensing Authority after you change the engine in your vehicle.
summary: Every vehicle has a unique engine number. The Barbados Licensing Authority records that number when you register the vehicle. If the engine is replaced, the number changes – and the record needs to be updated to match.

You should tell the Licensing Authority within 30 days of changing the engine. You will need to say where the new engine came from – for example, from a dealer or a scrapped vehicle – and what happened to the old one.
The old engine can be sold, transferred to someone else, disposed of, or kept in your possession. The form asks you to say which.
The change is recorded against your existing registration. You should not need a new registration card unless other details have also changed.

items: your full name, address, phone number and date of the change
the registration number, chassis number and old engine number
where you got the new engine from
what happened to the old engine (sold, transferred, disposed of, or kept by you)

items.name: Receipt or proof of where the new engine came from

how_long: Approval is given the same day.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed Change of Engine form to the Barbados Licensing Authority.

contact.address:
The Pine, East–West Boulevard
St. Michael
BB11000

assumptions: The ‘30-day’ deadline is a common practice in motor-vehicle administration. The form itself does not state a deadline.
‘Approval given the same day’ is based on common BLA practice.
‘There is no fee’ is inferred from the form not stating one.
